name: paraqus
documentation:
# Paraqus

Paraqus is a Python package developed to export simulation results to the VTK file format. 

## Documentation

The documentation for Paraqus can be found [here](https://paraqus.readthedocs.io/). It also contains information on how to install the software.


## License

Paraqus is released under the [GNU GPLv3](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/gpl-3.0/) license.

classes.name: paraqus.abaqus.abaqustools.OdbObject
documentation:
Context manager for Abaqus ODB objects.

Opens an ODB and closes it after we are done with it. If any
exceptions are raised while the ODB is open, it is still closed
afterwards.

# Parameters

file_name

: Path to the Abaqus .odb file.

readonly

: If `True` writing to the ODB is prohibited. Default is
  `True`.

# Attributes

file_path

: Absolute path to the .odb file.

readonly

: If `True` writing to the ODB is prohibited.

already_open

: A flag indicating wheter the ODB is already open or not.

modules.name: paraqus.tests
documentation:
Unit tests for Paraqus.

The unit tests are grouped in two separate modules.
The module paraqus.tests.tests_common contains all tests that are independent
of Abaqus, while the module paraqus.tests.tests_abaqus contains tests that
need the Abaqus Python interpreter to work.

To run the tests, use the functions `run_abaqus_tests()` or
`run_python_tests()`, respectively. Note that `run_abaqus_tests()`
also includes the tests that do not need Abaqus.

functions.name: paraqus.tests.run_abaqus_tests
documentation:
Run all unit tests using Abaqus Python.

Test results are exported to a text file `test_report_abaqus.txt` in the
current working directory.

If you are using a python interpreter outside of Abaqus, use the function
`run_python()` instead.

# Raises

RuntimeError

: If this function is not able to import modules from Abaqus.

# Returns

None.

functions.name: paraqus.tests.run_python_tests
documentation:
Run all unit tests using standard Python.

Test results are exported to a text file `test_report_python.txt` in the
current working directory.

This function runs only the subset of tests that does not need Abaqus to
work. If you use Abaqus, use the function `run_abaqus()` in the Abaqus
Python interpreter instead.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.paraqusmodel.ParaqusModel.add_field
documentation:
Add a field to the model.

The field will also be part of any exported VTK model.

# Parameters

field_name

: Name of the field.

field_tags

: Tags of the nodes or elements at which the field is stored.

field_values

: Values of the field in the order defined for the field tags.
  At nodes or elements that are not part of the field tags,
  the values will be set to `NaN` automatically.

field_position

: A constant defining the storage position of the field, i.e.
  `NODES` or `ELEMENTS`.

field_type

: A constant defining the field type, i.e. `SCALAR`,
  `VECTOR` or `TENSOR`.

# Returns

None.

might have holes etc.).

methods.name: paraqus.paraqusmodel.ParaqusModel._pad_field_values
documentation: Extend a field value array with nan values for unset tags.

Paraqus representation of a finite element model.

The Paraqus model can be used to manipulate and export any finite
element model as a set of VTK files.

# Parameters

element_tags

: Tags of all elements being part of the model. Tags have to be
  unique.

connectivity

: Connectivity list in the order defined for the element tags. The
  respective nodes have to be in order as defined for VTK cells.

element_types

: The VTK cell types for all elements in the order defined for the
  element tags.

node_tags

: Tags of all nodes being part of the model. Tags have to be
  unique.

node_coords

: Nodal coordinates in the order defined for the node tags. The
  coordinates can be defined in 1d-, 2d- or 3d-space as an array
  of shape (number of nodes, number of dimensions).

model_name

: Name of the main model. In case of multiple frames or parts
  every Paraqus model should have the same model name. Default:
  `'MODEL NAME'`.

part_name

: Name of the part instance. Default: `'PART NAME'`.

step_name

: Name of the load step. Default: `'STEP NAME'`.

frame_time

: Current frame time. Default: `0.0`.

source

: This is just for informational purpose and defines the source
  of the model, e.g. `ABAQUS`. Default: `USER`.

# Attributes

model_name

: Name of the main model.

part_name

: Name of the part instance.

step_name

: Name of the load step.

frame_time

: Current frame time of the model frame.

source

: This is a constant for informational purpose and describes the
  source of the model, e.g. `ABAQUS` or `USER`.

elements

: A repository storing all information on elements, e.g. tags and
  element types.

nodes

: Repository storing all information on nodes, e.g. tags and
  coordinates.

node_fields

: Repository storing all node fields.

element_fields

: Repository sotring all element fields.

# Example

```pycon
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from paraqus import ParaqusModel, BinaryWriter
>>>
>>> # Define the model parameters
>>> element_tags = np.array([1,2])
>>> connectivity = [np.array([1,2,3,4]),np.array([4,3,5])]
>>> element_types = np.array([9,5])
>>> node_tags = np.array([1,2,3,4,5])
>>> node_coords = np.array([[0,0],[1,0],[1,1],[0,1],[0.5,1.5]])
>>>
>>> # Create a new model
>>> model = ParaqusModel(element_tags,
>>>                      connectivity,
>>>                      element_types,
>>>                      node_tags,
>>>                      node_coords,
>>>                      model_name="foo",
>>>                      part_name="bar",
>>>                      step_name="foobar",
>>>                      frame_time=0.1)
>>>
>>> # Export model as VTK
>>> writer = BinaryWriter()
>>> writer.write(model)
```

Reads an .odb file and exports selected results to a ParaqusModel.

# Parameters

odb_path

: Name of the underlying ODB.

model_name

: Name of the model returned by the reader. When `model_name` is
  omitted, it is set based on `odb_path`. Default: `None`.

instance_names

: Instances that will be exported to individual models. If
  `instance_names` is omitted, all instances in the ODB will be
  exported. Default: `None`.

time_offset

: Assume the simulation started at this time when exporting.
  Useful to create files in VTK format that are ordered in time
  from multiple ODBs. Default: `0.0`.

# Attributes

odb_path

: Path to the underlying ODB.

model_name

: Name of the model returned by the reader.

field_export_requests

: Contains one request per field that will be exported to VTK
  format.

group_export_requests

: Contains one request per node or element set that will be
  exported.

instance_names

: Instances that will be exported to individual models.

time_offset

: Assume the simulation started at this time when exporting.
  Useful to create files in VTK format that are ordered in time
  from multiple ODBs.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from paraqus.abaqus import OdbReader
>>> # Define the odb/model/instances that will be read
>>> reader = OdbReader(odb_path="example.odb",
>>>                    model_name="example model",
>>>                    instance_names=["instance-1", "instance-2"],
>>>                    )
>>> # Export the node field 'U' (displacements)
>>> reader.add_field_export_request("U", field_position="nodes")
>>> # Export the element set 'element set name' for 'instance-1'
>>> reader.add_set_export_request("element set name",
>>>                               set_type="elements",
>>>                               instance_name="instance-1")
>>> # Store the models for both instances in a list
>>> instance_models = list(reader.read_instances(step_name="Step-1",
>>>                                              frame_index=-1)
>>>                        )
```

classes.name: paraqus.abaqus.odbreader.FieldExportRequest
documentation:
Specify a field output that will be exported.

# Parameters

field_name

: Abaqus identifier for the field, e.g. `'S'` for stress.

field_position

: What type of field. valid values: `'nodes'` or `'elements'`.

invariant

: Invariant of the field, if applicable, e.g. `'mises'` or
  `'magnitude'`. Default: `None`.

section_point_number

: Section point for shell elements. If no section point number is
  specified, the section point values are reduced according to
  `section_point_reduction`. Default: `None`.

section_point_reduction

: How to reduce values of multiple section points for the same
  node or element. Valid values are `'mean'` or `'absmax'`.
  Default: `'absmax'`.

# Attributes

  specified, the section point values are reduced according to
  `section_point_reduction`.

Context manager for VTK file reading and writing.

Can handle all kinds of supported VTK files, i.e. .vtu, .pvtu and
.pvd files. Files can only be opened in write mode.

# Parameters

file_name

: The name of the VTK file that will be written.

fmt

: Constant defining the output format of array data, i.e.
  `ASCII` or `BINARY`.

# Attributes

file_path

: The absolute path to the VTK file.

fmt

: Constant defining the output format of array data, i.e.
  `ASCII` or `BINARY`.

# Example

```pycon
>>> from paraqus.constants import BINARY
>>> with VtkFileManager("my_file.vtu", BINARY) as vtu_file:
>>>     vtu_file.write("<VTKFile>\n")
>>>     vtu_file.write("<UnstructuredGrid>\n")
>>>     vtu_file.write("</UnstructuredGrid>\n")
>>>     vtu_file.write("</VTKFile>\n")
```

methods.name: paraqus.writers.BinaryWriter._write_vtu_file
documentation:
Write a .vtu file for a piece or submodel of a paraqus model.

# Parameters

piece

: The piece or submodel to export.

piece_tag

: The identifier of the currently processed piece. Default:
  `0`.

# Returns

file_path

: The path to the exported .vtu file.

Writer for the export of paraqus models to binary .vtu file format.

# Parameters

output_dir

: Directory, where all exported VTK files will be stored. Default:
  `'vtk_files'`.

clear_output_dir

: If `True`, the output directory will be cleared before
  exporting any files. Default: `False`.

number_of_pieces

: Number of pieces each model will be split into. Default: `1`.

encoding

: The binary encoding used for data arrays. Currently supported
  are `RAW` and `BASE64`. Default: `BASE64`.

header_type

: The data type used for the headers of the binary data blocks.
  Currently supported are `UINT32` and `UINT64`. Default:
  `UINT64`.

# Attributes

number_of_pieces

: Number of pieces each model will be split into.

output_dir

: The path to the folder where all VTK files will be stored.

encoding

: The binary encoding used for data arrays.

header_type

: The data type used for the headers of the binary data blocks.

FORMAT

: This is a constant with value BINARY and is only used for
  informational purposes.

# Example

```pycon
>>> from paraqus import BinaryWriter
>>> from paraqus.constants import RAW
>>> writer = BinaryWriter(number_of_pieces=2, encoding=RAW)
>>> writer.write(random_paraqus_model)
```

: If `True`, a linebreak will be inserted after each closed
  element section. Default: `True`.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.writers.XmlFactory.add_and_finish_element
documentation:
Add an element section and close it immediately.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the element section.

break_line

: If `True`, a linebreak will be inserted after each element
  section. Default: `True`.

attributes

: Attributes of the element section. The key will be the
  name of the attribute, the value will be the value of the
  attribute.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.writers.XmlFactory.add_content_to_element
documentation:
Add any content that is not a array-shaped to the XML file.

# Parameters

content

: The content to add.

break_line

: If `True`, a linebreak will be inserted after the content
  has been written. Default: `True`.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.writers.XmlFactory.add_array_data_to_element
documentation:
Add array data to the XML file.

# Parameters

array

: The array data to add.

break_line

: If `True`, a linebreak will be inserted after the array
  data. Default: `True`.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.writers.XmlFactory._write_binary_array_data
documentation:
Add binary encoded array data to the XML file.

# Parameters

array

: The array to add to the output file.

break_line

: If `True`, a linebreak will be inserted after the array
  data has been written. Default: `True`.

# Returns

None.

methods.name: paraqus.writers.XmlFactory._write_ascii_array_data
documentation:
Add array data in ASCII format to the XML file.

# Parameters

array

: The array to add to the output file.

line_break

: If `True`, a line break will be inserted after each line
  of the array. Default: `True`.

# Returns

None.

Factory to produce properly formatted XML files.

# Parameters

stream

: The output stream of the file that is written.

encoding

: The binary encoding used for data arrays. Currently supported
  are `RAW` and `BASE64`. This is not needed in case of
  writing VTK ASCII files. Default: `None`.

header_type

: The data type used for the headers of the binary data blocks.
  Currently supported are `UINT32` and `UINT64`. This is not
  needed in case of writing VTK ASCII files. Default: `None`.
